content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 9, 2006

Neal Kelley
Registrar of Voters
1300 South Grande Avenue, Building C
Santa Ana, California 92704

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-06-102

Dear Mr. Kelley:

Chairman Randolph has asked me to respond to your letter of May 24, 2006. Since your
letter concerns the alleged past conduct by another person (the failure to report an economic
interest in a business) I have referred it to the Commission's Enforcement Division for review
and determination of whether any violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred. You may
contact the Enforcement Division directly at (916) 322-6441, if you have any questions about this
matter.

In addition, you also noted that you believed that the subject of your letter, a candidate
for assembly, did not meet the residence requirement necessary to run for nomination in the
district. However, the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act ("Act")¹. We cannot provide advice with regard to provisions such as candidate
residency requirements which are not covered by the Act.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:rd

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a candidate for assembly who allegedly failed to report an economic interest in a business violate the Political Reform Act, and does the candidate meet residency requirements to run in the district?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC refers the matter regarding the alleged failure to report an economic interest to the Enforcement Division for review. The Commission cannot provide advice on residency requirements as they are not covered by the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document is a response letter addressing two issues: enforcement referral for alleged economic interest reporting violation, and declination of jurisdiction regarding residency requirements. Letter structured as direct response to May 24, 2006 inquiry.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a candidate for assembly who allegedly failed to report an economic interest in a business violate the Political Reform Act, and does the candidate meet residency requirements to run in the district?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC refers the matter regarding the alleged failure to report an economic interest to the Enforcement Division for review. The Commission cannot provide advice on residency requirements as they are not covered by the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request from the Registrar of Voters regarding a candidate for assembly, referencing a referral to the Enforcement Division for investigation of an alleged failure to report an economic interest, and declining jurisdiction over residency requirement questions outside the Political Reform Act.
